---
title: Deep Learning 2
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: _extensions/clean/clean.scss
filters:
  - fontawesome
engine: julia
execute:
  cache: true
---





## An even simpler example: A Line

* Extreme case: 1 input, 1 output. 
* (Of course we don't *need* a neural network for that)

```{julia}
#| echo: true
using DataFrames
truth(x) = 3 + 2x  # true DGP
# x for training and testing
# notice: those are row vectors!
x_train, x_test = hcat(0:5...), hcat(6:10...)
# y too
y_train, y_test = truth.(x_train), truth.(x_test)
# dataframe looks nicer
```


## A line

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
### Training Data

```{julia}
DataFrame(x_train = x_train[:],y_train = y_train[:])
```
:::
::: {.column}
### Test Data

```{julia}
DataFrame(x_test = x_test[:],y_test = y_test[:])
```
:::
:::
<!-- end columns -->

## A Line

```{julia}
#| echo: true
using Flux

# 1 layer, with 1 input and 1 output.
model = Dense(1 => 1) 

@show model.weight
@show model.bias
```

Let's get a second object to predict outputs:

```{julia}
#| echo: true
predict = Dense(1 => 1)
predict(x_train)  # garbage
```

## A Line - Improving the Quality of Prediction

<!-- https://rajgoel.github.io/course-machine-learning/?topic=02-lecture -->
